PHILIP COHEN 
A ttorney at Law 
266 Mobil Ave., Suite 109 
Camarillo, California 93010 
(805) 484-3866 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
State of California 
P.O. Box 807 
1100 IIKII Street Building 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Attn: 
r~r. Ken Goshorn 
Re: 
Conflict of Interest 
Gov. Code Sees. 87300 et. seq. 
Dear Mr. Goshorn: 
November 15, 1977 
In accordance with our discussion earlier today, please 
be advised that this office is legal counsel to the Ventura-Santa 
Barbara Health Systems Agency. 
Our review of relevant legislation 
pursuant to Conflicts of Interest, has resulted in the posing of 
the following question. 
Is the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency an 
IIAgencyll (Gov. Code Section 82003) for which a Conflict of Inter-
est Code (Gov. Code Sections 87300 et. seq.) must be promulgated 
and submitted to the code reviewing body (Fair Political Practices 
Commission) in accordance with Government Code, Sections 82011 and 
87303). 
On the one hand, it appears that the State of California 
requires a Conflict of Interest Code drafted pursuant to the re-
quirements of the Fair Political Practices Commission. 
On the 
other hand, my discussion with you left me with the impression 
that the Commission has not yet implemented a procedure relative 
to entities such as the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency, 
which is a non-profit corporation. 
Although I am in the process of drafting a conflict of in-
terest code for the Agency, and will of course submit it to the 
Commission for appropriate action, I would very much appreciate 
the response of the Commission to the question posed herein. 
Very 
PC:bka

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency, a non-profit corporation, qualifies as an "agency" under Government Code Section 82003 and is therefore required to promulgate and submit a Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated November 15, 1977) from Cohen and the FPPC response letter (dated 1977, specific date unclear due to OCR). The response letter indicates this is correspondence acknowledging receipt of the request, stating staff will combine it with another similar request (Opinion Request No. 77-043) and requesting additional information before providing advice. OCR quality affects clarity of some dates and text portions.

embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming request from Philip Cohen, legal counsel to the Ventura-Santa Barbara Health Systems Agency, asking whether the Agency must adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under California Government Code Sections 87300 et seq. The FPPC response letter indicates the staff is combining this request with a similar opinion request (No. 77-043) and needs additional information before determining whether Health Systems Agencies are subject to these requirements, as prior staff advice had indicated they were not.
